articles.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
articles.description: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

articles.title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
articles.description: In the coming weeks, Apple will move anonymously generated emails addresses to a different domain.

articles.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
articles.description: SpaceX's valuation has increased by $1 trillion since its shares started trading on Friday.

articles.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
articles.description: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said Tuesday that the company is working on over 40 different AI wearable devices — including jewelry, earbuds with cameras, pins, and watches — a sign of how aggressively the chipmaker is betting that the next major computing platform won't be a phone.

articles.title: Bug in FIFA World Cup internal system gave anyone ability to modify TV stream
articles.description: A security researcher said a flaw in FIFA’s online platforms allowed her to access several internal systems, including one that could have allowed her to take control of the TV stream of every World Cup match.

articles.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
articles.description: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

articles.title: Mobileye’s US robotaxi launch will put it on both sides of the AV business
articles.description: Mobileye apparently wants to own some of the robotaxi market, even if that puts it in direct competition with companies it supplies its self-driving system to.

articles.title: Rivian cuts hundreds of workers after R2 deliveries start
articles.description: The company said the cuts were part of a restructuring meant to help scale to profitability. Rivian recently pushed back its profitability goal to invest in autonomy.

articles.title: Snap finally debuts its long-awaited AR glasses, Specs, and, oof, they aren’t cheap
articles.description: For over a decade now, Snap has been working on this device. Now the glasses are finally here. So what stands out on first impression?

articles.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
articles.description: WordPress VIP’s latest survey suggests consumers are wary of AI-generated answers even as companies increasingly view AI search as an important referral channel.

articles.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
articles.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

articles.title: India orders temporary ban on Telegram over exam fraud concerns
articles.description: The restrictions include a nationwide ban on Telegram until June 22 and a requirement to disable the app's message-editing feature.

articles.title: Payments startup Flutterwave hits $3.2B valuation, backed by Ripple
articles.description: African payments infrastructure company Flutterwave has hit a new valuation and landed blockchain company Ripple as an investor and partner.

articles.title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
articles.description: The Justice department says the Pentagon needs xAI to keep using its unpermitted gas turbines.

articles.title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
articles.description: Plaud is trying to make a mark in a crowded market full of AI-powered meeting notetakers.

articles.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
articles.description: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

articles.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
articles.description: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

articles.title: This startup’s super metals could soon be in military drones, luxury watches, and chef’s knives
articles.description: Instead of heating metals, Foundation Alloy beats them into submission. The startup has raised $22 million to scale up production of its alloys.

articles.title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
articles.description: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

articles.title: Threads adds new personalization and community features as it reaches 500M monthly users
articles.description: The Meta-owned social platform announced a series of new features launching today, including a "Your Algo" tool that lets users control what they see in their feeds.

articles.title: Cockroaches scurry around with thousands of pieces of bacterial genomes
articles.description: Transferring genes across species doesn't just happen in microbes.

articles.title: Among the large new rockets Amazon was counting on, only Europe has delivered
articles.description: "As for Arianespace, they have definitely stepped up."

articles.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
articles.description: Move originally planned for Monday would have heavily increased power users' costs.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **Anthropic与特朗普政府的冲突或成利好**：销售数据显示，Anthropic近期与特朗普政府的争端可能反而对其业务产生积极影响。
2. **SpaceX估值飙升至2.6万亿美元**：SpaceX估值短暂超过亚马逊，达到2.6万亿美元，并已公开上市（IPO），引发市场关注。
3. **Android 17发布与Gemini功能扩展**：谷歌推出Android 17，新增多任务工具，并进一步扩展Gemini AI功能。
4. **Snap发布AR眼镜Specs，但价格高昂**：Snap终于推出其长期研发的增强现实眼镜Specs，但售价不菲，市场反应待观察。
5. **美国六成消费者反感品牌使用“AI”宣传**：调查显示，60%的美国消费者认为品牌营销中提及“AI”会让人反感，提示企业需谨慎使用AI标签。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：SpaceX估值突破2.6万亿美元并完成上市，成为科技与航天领域里程碑式事件，同时美国消费者对“AI”营销的普遍抵触情绪也为品牌策略敲响警钟。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **模型开发与评估工具**：Hugging Face发布了“olmo-eval”评估工作台，用于优化模型开发循环；同时推出PyTorch性能分析指南，聚焦从nn.Linear到融合MLP的优化。
2. **OpenAI生态与合作拓展**：OpenAI推出合作伙伴网络、新学院课程，并宣布收购Ona；多家企业如BBVA、Preply、LSEG等利用OpenAI模型推动金融、教育和个性化学习创新。
3. **AI部署与行为预测**：OpenAI提出通过模拟部署来预测模型发布前行为，并支持欧洲构建可信AI生态系统；同时披露PRC关联影响力行动试图干预美国AI辩论。
4. **社区投资与能源可负担性**：Google在阿拉巴马州和弗吉尼亚州新增投资与社区支持，旨在创造本地就业并扩大能源可负担性。
5. **跨领域AI应用案例**：天体物理学家利用Codex模拟黑洞；Oracle云承诺支持访问OpenAI模型和Codex，推动数据到决策的AI规模化。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI密集推出合作伙伴网络、新课程及收购计划，同时强化对模型部署前行为预测与欧洲AI治理的支持，显示其正加速构建从技术到生态的全链条布局。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中方驳斥菲律宾与日本划界言论**：中国专家批评菲律宾外长称日菲海上划界“与中国无关”的说法，指出此举别有用心，超出双边范畴，意在服务其他战略目的。
2. **中美科技与贸易摩擦持续**：美国要求追回已售华的美军电视墙制造商设备；美国联网汽车新规迫使福特等公司为在华生产车型申请许可；中国禁止销售德国英飞凌氮化镓芯片，并加强对磷化铟等关键材料出口管控。
3. **中国产业与能源战略调整**：菲律宾成为中国太阳能板第二大市场；中国推进大型核反应堆建设；通过减少石油进口支撑全球经济，并加速量子通信安全领域布局。
4. **科技竞争与监管动向**：中国在量子通信安全领域领先；微软因中美数据法律收紧缩减大陆云业务岗位；中方打击西方AI模型，同时美国企业涌入中国深度求索（DeepSeek）平台。
5. **教育与社会动态**：中国高考制度正在改革；美国SpaceX模式或影响中国IPO雄心，但技术差距仍存。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中方在南海划界问题上强硬回击菲律宾与日本勾连，同时中美在芯片、汽车、数据等领域的科技与贸易博弈持续升级，中国正通过关键材料出口管制与自主核能、量子技术布局强化战略自主。
